title: MCP server — Private Data Ops
description: Tool surface for the canonical Private Data Ops MCP server. Exposes read-only access to Private DB rows (groups + their members joined to Shared DB display fields) so an AI client can answer 'who's in this group' and 'find my X group' questions. Members are returned with orphan-tolerant nulls when a member's record_id no longer resolves in the Shared DB after a re-mirror (per AC-10). AC-MCP-A applies: cloud AI clients require explicit per-call consent before invoking tools on this server. v1 scope is groups only; tag/note CRUD will appear in a later toolkit version once users are writing those at scale. See spec/PNA_Spec.md § Universal architectural commitments (AC-MCP-A, AC-10) and § Vision (canonical MCP servers).

server_name.description: Stable identifier the AI client wires up in its MCP config.

transport.description: JSON-RPC frames over stdin/stdout, per the MCP transport contract for desktop AI clients.

tool_list_groups.description: List all groups in the Private DB, ordered by most-recently-updated first.

total.description: Total groups in the Private DB, pre-limit.

tool_find_group.description: Find groups whose name contains the given substring (case-insensitive). Useful when the user asks for 'the climate group' — match on substring, return whatever's there. If multiple match, the AI client disambiguates by showing the names or by calling get_group_members on each.

name.description: Substring to match against group names. Trimmed; empty returns an empty result set (use list_groups instead).

query.description: Echo of the trimmed query that ran.

tool_get_group_members.description: Fetch one group plus its members, joined to Shared DB display fields in a single round-trip. Members whose record_id no longer resolves in the Shared DB after a re-mirror come back with null name/contact_email/fellow_type/currently_based_in — record_id is preserved so the workspace's orphan preview (AC-10) can identify what would be lost.

group_id.description: Numeric group id from list_groups / find_group.

oneOf.description: No group exists with that id.

GroupSummary.description: One group's identity + counts, without the member list. Shared by list_groups, find_group, and get_group_members responses.

group_id.description: Stable numeric id from the Private DB.

note.description: Free-text user note attached to the group. Empty string when unset.

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp; rebuckets ordering in list_groups.

Member.description: One member of a group. record_id comes from the Private DB; the remaining fields are joined from the Shared DB and become null when the record_id no longer resolves there (orphan after re-mirror, per AC-10).
